subject: HSC Physics
description: Index of marking guidelines by year. Each entry references the full marking guideline data embedded in the question bank. This file provides a quick lookup for the marking-guideline-feedback skill.

partialCreditPolicy.description: OpenTutor partial credit assessment policy, aligned with NESA principles

rules.condition: Correct method but arithmetic or unit conversion error
Correct first step but incorrect subsequent steps
Correct answer without required working shown
Follow-through error: error from part (a) carried correctly into part (b)
Correct magnitude but incorrect or missing direction for a vector quantity
Correct method but incorrect significant figures or rounding
rules.marksAwarded: full marks minus 1 (once per question only)

generalPrinciples: Marks are awarded for correct physical reasoning and working, not just the final answer
Where a question says 'show that', full working must be shown — the answer is given
Correct answer without working may receive zero marks for multi-mark questions
If a student makes an error early but carries through consistently, follow-through marks may be awarded
Diagrams (free body diagrams, ray diagrams, circuit diagrams) must be clearly labelled to receive associated marks
Vector quantities require both magnitude and direction for full marks
Units must be included in all final answers — missing units lose marks
Rounding: round only at the final step unless otherwise instructed
Scientific notation must be used for very large or very small numbers

sectionI.marking: No working required. 1 mark for correct answer, 0 otherwise.

sectionII.description: Extended response questions requiring full working
sectionII.marking: Criteria-based marking. See individual question entries in questions.json for full criteria.
generalNotes: Show all necessary working including formula, substitution and evaluation steps
Marks may be awarded for correct intermediate steps even if the final answer is wrong
State conclusions and interpret results in the context of the physical problem
For 'explain' or 'describe' questions, refer to relevant physical laws or principles by name

2023-PH-Q11b.keySkill: work done by an angled force

2023-PH-Q15.keySkill: circular motion at top of hill — normal force and minimum speed

2023-PH-Q17.keySkill: photoelectric effect — work function and KE_max

2023-PH-Q19.keySkill: transformer turns ratio and RMS to peak voltage

2023-PH-Q20.keySkill: orbital speed, period and explanation of orbital mechanics

2023-PH-Q21.keySkill: alpha decay equation, half-life and activity

2023-PH-Q22.keySkill: quark composition and standard model classification
